# Copilot Instructions

## Repository Purpose

This repository serves as a **public proof of existence and authorship** for the **Theory of Fundamental Belief (TFB)**, an original theoretical framework authored by Christian Montgomery. It provides stable identifiers, public documentation, and machine-readable metadata for academic reference and indexing.

## Project Context

- **Theory**: Theory of Fundamental Belief (TFB) / Teoria do Campo Fundamental (TCF in Portuguese)
- **Author**: Christian Montgomery (ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0009-5364-249X)
- **Official site**: https://tfbtheory.com
- **DOI**: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17991356
- **Wikidata**: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q137856487

## Repository Structure

- `index.html` — English version of the public documentation page (GitHub Pages)
- `index-pt.html` — Portuguese version of the public documentation page
- `README.md` — Repository overview and official identifiers
- `PUBLIC_STATEMENT.md` — Bilingual public statement of authorship existence
- `TFB_Conceptual_Framework_v1.3.pdf` — Conceptual framework document
- `audio-calibration.md` — Audio calibration documentation

## Content Guidelines

- This is a **static documentation repository** — there is no build system, package manager, or test suite.
- All content changes should preserve the academic and formal tone of the existing documentation.
- The repository is bilingual (English and Portuguese); updates to English content should be mirrored in the Portuguese version where applicable.
- Do **not** introduce frameworks, dependencies, or build tooling unless explicitly requested.
- Preserve all DOI links, ORCID identifiers, Wikidata references, and other stable academic identifiers exactly as they appear.
- When editing HTML files, maintain the existing inline style and structure — no external CSS frameworks.

## Writing Style

- Formal, academic tone in both English and Portuguese sections.
- Content must be factually accurate and consistent with the TFB theoretical framework.
- Do not alter statements of authorship, priority, or intellectual property.
